title: Nephrodium velleum
taxonomy: Nephrodium velleum is now classified as a species of fern, specifically within the family Dryopteridaceae. This classification is based on its characteristics and relationships with other fern species. As a member of the Dryopteridaceae family, Nephrodium velleum is closely related to other genera such as Dryopteris and Polystichum. The family Dryopteridaceae is part of the larger division Polypodiophyta, which includes all ferns. Understanding the classification of Nephrodium velleum is essential for its identification and study in the field of botany.
uses: Nephrodium velleum, also known as the lady fern or the fragile fern, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The rhizome and roots of this plant have been used to treat different health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin problems. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to the presence of certain compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects.
